MARKETS INITIALLY PANICKED OVER THE STRAIT CLOSURE, BUT TRUMP'S SURPRISINGLY OPTIMISTIC RHETORIC ("GOING ALONG VERY WELL," POSSIBILITY OF MORE TALKS) SHIFTED SENTIMENT. 
THE PATTERN SHOWS: INITIAL FEAR → REASSESSMENT → RISK-ON RECOVERY, BUT WITH ENOUGH UNCERTAINTY REMAINING TO KEEP GOLD SUPPORTED AND OIL ELEVATED.

IRAN REARMS FAST DESPITE DAMAGE CLAIMS

IRAN SAYS IT IS REBUILDING ITS MISSILE AND DRONE STOCKPILES DURING THE CEASEFIRE FASTER THAN BEFORE THE WAR, DESPITE US CLAIMS ITS DEFENSES WERE HEAVILY DEGRADED.

“DURING THE CEASEFIRE PERIOD, OUR PACE IN UPGRADING AND REFILLING MISSILE AND DRONE LAUNCH PLATFORMS IS EVEN FASTER THAN BEFORE THE WAR,” SAID IRGC AEROSPACE FORCE COMMANDER BRIGADIER GENERAL MOUSAVI, VIA STATE BROADCASTER IRIB. “WE KNOW THE ENEMY IS UNABLE TO CREATE SIMILAR CONDITIONS FOR ITSELF AND HAS TO BRING IN MUNITIONS FROM ACROSS THE WORLD BIT BY BIT,” HE ADDED, ALSO CLAIMING THE US HAS LOST CONTROL OF THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ, LEBANON, AND THE REGION.

EARLIER THIS MONTH, US AIR FORCE GEN. DAN CAINE SAID CENTCOM HAD “DESTROYED APPROXIMATELY 80% OF IRAN’S AIR DEFENSE SYSTEMS,” HITTING OVER 1,500 AIR DEFENSE TARGETS, 450 MISSILE STORAGE SITES, AND 800 DRONE FACILITIES. “ALL OF THESE SYSTEMS ARE GONE,” HE ASSERTED.

HOWEVER, INTELLIGENCE SOURCES TOLD CNN THAT ROUGHLY HALF OF IRAN’S MISSILE LAUNCHERS REMAIN INTACT AND THOUSANDS OF ONE-WAY ATTACK DRONES ARE STILL IN ITS ARSENAL, SUGGESTING A MORE MIXED PICTURE. ...
